mcqs.question: What is the primary goal of Multiple Linear Regression?

options.text: To reduce the dimensionality of data

mcqs.explanation: Multiple Linear Regression is used to predict a continuous outcome based on multiple predictor variables.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an assumption of Multiple Linear Regression?
mcqs.explanation: Independence is an assumption of Multiple Linear Regression, meaning that each observation is independent of the others.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of R-squared in Multiple Linear Regression?

options.text: To evaluate the goodness of fit of the model
To select the best predictors for the model
To detect multicollinearity in the data

mcqs.explanation: R-squared measures the goodness of fit of the model, indicating how well the model explains the variation in the outcome variable.

mcqs.question: Which type of regularization is used to prevent overfitting in Multiple Linear Regression?
mcqs.explanation: Both L1 and L2 regularization can be used to prevent overfitting in Multiple Linear Regression.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between Multiple Linear Regression and Simple Linear Regression?

options.text: Multiple Linear Regression has multiple predictor variables, while Simple Linear Regression has only one predictor variable
Multiple Linear Regression has a single predictor variable, while Simple Linear Regression has multiple predictor variables
Multiple Linear Regression is used for categorical outcomes, while Simple Linear Regression is used for continuous outcomes
Multiple Linear Regression is used for continuous outcomes, while Simple Linear Regression is used for categorical outcomes

mcqs.explanation: Multiple Linear Regression has multiple predictor variables, while Simple Linear Regression has only one predictor variable.
